what_it_does: This file establishes the founding design mandate for the autonomous build of the Company's real front-end instrument surface, capturing Tim Geldard's verbatim requirements and expanding them into laws, mission, scope, design language, completion criteria, and a loop-prep build method.
observations: Declares itself gospel/law for the complete autonomous build of the real front end.
Contains verbatim source-of-truth messages from Tim Geldard dated 2026-06-14.
Status note says the doc is captured+expanded design, NOT ratified, with [EXTENSION] markers.
Derives laws L1-L11 directly from each line of Tim's messages.
Reframes the surface as a universal, data-source-agnostic instrument with a first-class project system; the Company is the starter project.
Preserves only the address-on-UI + attached context pattern from the existing UI; everything else is disposable.
Introduces a registry-promotion lifecycle where nucleated candidates are added to registries via operator-driven proposal/approval.
Specifies three first-class form factors: desktop, mobile-portrait, and mobile-landscape.
Defines a fresh paper-like design language: soft shadows, polygons, pigment meaning, shared motion system, disclosure grammar.
Lists systems and data to integrate: instrument bridge, real data counts, live SSE spine, address substrate, multipurpose strata.
Sets a ten-item completion bar verified by actual browser use and separate design critique.
Includes open questions to be resolved by showing Tim renders, not by asking him to spec.
Explicit constraint: spawned agents must not arm their own autonomous self-modifying loops.
standouts: Tim's verbatim words are the unbreakable source of truth; expansions are marked [EXTENSION] and open to correction.
The front end is declared half the build with no ceiling on quality.
The instrument must be native and equally refined on desktop, mobile-portrait, and mobile-landscape for everything.
A project system makes the universal instrument pointable at any data source; the Company's data is only the starter project.
Nucleated registry candidates can be promoted into real types through the existing proposal/approval lifecycle while the instrument stays read-only.
Address-on-UI + attached context is the sole inheritance from the current UI; the old harness must be ignored.
conventions: Uses hierarchical numbered sections §0-§8 with subsections.
Verbatim quotes in blockquotes; author expansions tagged [EXTENSION].
Each law follows statement / why / verify structure.
Heavy ALL CAPS emphasis for load-bearing terms.
Self-aware meta-commentary about status, authorship, and scope.
Breaks concise README norms by declaring size a feature and being intentionally expansive.
concerns: Status is captured+expanded but NOT ratified, so much content is speculative pending Tim correction.
Large volume of [EXTENSION] material risks blurring founder-mandated vs author-inferred requirements.
Scope is extremely broad: all form factors, all data, all lenses, project system, registry promotion, design system, and build method in one mandate.
Verification criteria assume a separate design-critic workflow whose mechanics are not defined in this file.
No ceiling and size-as-feature may resist scoping and acceptance thresholds.
notes: This is the gospel charter that turns Tim Geldard's front-end vision into an expansive, self-aware build mandate covering laws, a universal project-pointable surface, paper-like design language, and loop-prep method.
questions: What is the exact mechanism for minting ui:// addresses and attaching context?
Where exactly are the 'heaps processed... somewhere' of Company data located beyond the approximate counts?
What is the current proposal/approval/execution lifecycle that registry promotion must reuse?
Which rendering substrate will be chosen for the seed geometry?
What are the precise token values for the fresh design language?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — it states it is a founding mandate/gospel for the front-end build and it is exactly that: a prescriptive, expanded design charter.
apparent_themes: No-ceiling quality via loop-prep and dynamic workflows
intention_signals: It is trying to create an authoritative, unambiguous creative brief that protects Tim's verbatim intent while expanding it into actionable design and architecture constraints, and to bootstrap a high-quality front-end build that is both beautiful and deeply integrated with the Company's systems and equation.
novelty: It treats a front-end mandate as gospel with verbatim source-of-truth and explicit [EXTENSION] markings; reframes the front end as a universal project-pointable instrument rather than a fixed dashboard; introduces a registry-promotion lifecycle that closes the data-to-type loop; and uses the equation's seed geometry as the literal visual language and layout grid.
contribution: This file contributes a rich, self-aware design charter that captures founder intent verbatim, derives concrete laws and completion criteria, and frames the front end as a universal, addressed, paper-aesthetic instrument surface integrated with the equation and data systems.
summary_for_embedding: company/build-prep/instrument-surface/MANDATE.md is an expansive founding build charter that records Tim Geldard's verbatim front-end vision and expands it into laws, a universal project-pointable instrument surface design, a paper-like motion-rich aesthetic, and a loop-prep build method for the Company's real interactive application.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to serve as the authoritative, ratification-pending gospel for autonomously building the Company's front-end instrument surface with no quality ceiling.
